compiled_state.title: RED: Write failing tests for config-driven import patterns in centrality scoring
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Modify tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-centrality.sh to add four new behavioral tests that verify record-test-status.sh reads import patterns from test_gate.import_pattern.<lang> config keys. All tests should FAIL (RED) before the implementation task.

### Tests to write

1. **test_config_pattern_used_for_centrality**: Create test repo with test_gate.import_pattern.bash=source $MODULE in .claude/dso-config.conf. Create 9+ bash files that use 'source module_name'. Stage a change to the source module. Run record-test-status.sh and verify centrality count exceeds threshold, triggering full suite.

2. **test_missing_config_patterns_falls_back_to_defaults**: Create test repo with NO test_gate.import_pattern.* keys in config. Create files using standard import/source patterns. Verify existing hardcoded grep patterns still count centrality correctly (backward compatibility).

3. **test_cross_language_isolation**: Create test repo with test_gate.import_pattern.python=import $MODULE configured (only Python pattern). Stage a bash file. Create 9+ Python files importing the module. Verify centrality is counted from the Python importers, NOT from bash source statements that happen to exist. This tests that language-specific patterns are used correctly.

4. **test_empty_config_pattern_falls_back**: Create test repo with test_gate.import_pattern.bash= (present but empty value). Verify the script falls back to hardcoded default patterns rather than running grep with an empty pattern.

### TDD setup
- Add RED marker to .test-index: plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh: tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-centrality.sh [test_config_pattern_used_for_centrality]
- Test approach: Each test creates an isolated git repo via create_test_repo(), stages files, runs the hook via bash, and asserts on test-gate-status output. Tests are behavioral (execute the hook and verify output), not structural.

### File Impact
- tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-centrality.sh (modify — add 4 test functions)
- .test-index (modify — add RED marker)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0) — RED-marked tests tolerated
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] ruff check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py 2>/dev/null || true
- [ ] test_config_pattern_used_for_centrality exists in test file
  Verify: grep -q 'test_config_pattern_used_for_centrality' tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-centrality.sh
- [ ] test_missing_config_patterns_falls_back_to_defaults exists in test file
  Verify: grep -q 'test_missing_config_patterns_falls_back_to_defaults' tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-centrality.sh
- [ ] test_cross_language_isolation exists in test file
  Verify: grep -q 'test_cross_language_isolation' tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-centrality.sh
- [ ] test_empty_config_pattern_falls_back exists in test file
  Verify: grep -q 'test_empty_config_pattern_falls_back' tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-centrality.sh
- [ ] RED marker present in .test-index
  Verify: grep -q 'record-test-status.*test-record-test-status-centrality.*\[test_config_pattern_used_for_centrality\]' .test-index
- [ ] All 4 new tests FAIL when run in isolation (RED state confirmed)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-centrality.sh 2>&1 | grep -c 'FAIL.*config_pattern\|FAIL.*cross_language\|FAIL.*empty_config\|FAIL.*falls_back_to_defaults'

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 6/6: Done ✓ — Files: tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-centrality.sh, .test-index. Tests: 16 pass, 2 RED (expected). Review: passed (all 5s).
comments.author: Test
